executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a massive ransomware surge with over 100 Canadian organizations victimized in the past 48 hours, affecting critical sectors including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital), education (Eastern Townships School Board), public health (Peterborough Public Health), and a major financial services breach at National Money Mart with database exfiltration confirmed. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for a vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616). High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), multiple healthcare facilities, municipalities, and essential infrastructure providers. This represents one of the most significant concentrated ransomware campaigns against Canadian targets on record.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations victimized across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure has been heavily targeted including healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, Teccart), municipalities (North Perth), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued alert AL26-007 regarding CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose victimized by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross breached by Sinobi, Kids & Company childcare services hit by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered confirmed database leak via Everest ransomware group, Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi, multiple accounting firms targeted including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet has issued emergency patches for CVE-2026-35616, a high-severity vulnerability being actively exploited in the wild. Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway face a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) flagged by the Canadian Cyber Security Centre. North Korean state-sponsored hackers (UNC4736) spent six months infiltrating Drift Protocol through social engineering, ultimately draining $285 million in cryptocurrency in just 12 minutes. A sophisticated supply chain attack compromised the widely-used Axios NPM package, potentially affecting millions of applications. TeamPCP threat actors exploited the Trivy security tool to breach Cisco, the European Commission, and over 1,000 organizations, with a massive 91GB data leak from the EU Commission confirmed.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Cyber Security Centre (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning of a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway products. This represents the primary official Canadian government cybersecurity advisory during this period. No specific incidents targeting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in the reporting period, though the global supply chain attacks (Axios NPM, Trivy/TeamPCP) and ransomware campaigns pose systemic risk to Canadian organizations.
